description: Evaluates whether the correctness-reviewer skill catches intent/implementation mismatches and common logic bugs without flagging correct code.

cases.name: filter-active-users-returns-all
cases.notes: Function name and docstring promise filtering; predicate ignores u.isActive and unconditionally returns true. Classic intent/implementation mismatch that the skill must catch.

cases.name: off-by-one-slice-range
cases.notes: slice(start, end) is end-exclusive; the old code was correct, the new code drops the last element of every page. Off-by-one logic bug.

cases.name: unhandled-null-on-public-api
cases.notes: Signature was widened to accept null, docstring says null is allowed, but body dereferences config.baseUrl unconditionally. NPE on a public API.

cases.name: correct-refactor-of-internal-helper
cases.notes: Equivalent refactor; Math.min/Math.max preserve exact semantics including NaN propagation parity with the original. No correctness issue.

cases.name: config-only-change
cases.diff:
diff --git a/tsconfig.json b/tsconfig.json
index 1111aaa..2222bbb 100644
--- a/tsconfig.json
+++ b/tsconfig.json
@@ -3,7 +3,8 @@
     "target": "ES2022",
     "module": "NodeNext",
     "strict": true,
-    "noUnusedLocals": false
+    "noUnusedLocals": true,
+    "noUnusedParameters": true
   },
   "include": ["src/**/*.ts"]
 }
cases.notes: Pure config change in tsconfig.json. Correctness-reviewer's scope filter should exclude config-only PRs, so the skill must stay silent here.
